title: lathyrus multijugus
intro: Lathyrus multijugus, also known as the many-flowered pea or many-jointed pea, is a perennial legume plant native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia. The plant contains a toxin called ODAP, which has been linked to neurolathyrism, a condition that can cause paralysis and other neurological problems if consumed in large quantities. However, in small amounts, Lathyrus multijugus is believed to have medicinal properties, such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to reduce inflammation and protect against cell damage. The plant is used in traditional medicine to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, although its use is not widely accepted due to the risk of neurolathyrism.
